---
title: "Design C: Multiple APIs via directory-convention workspace"
parentIssue: 1718
labels:
  - enhancement
  - design
assignees: []
milestone:
---

This is one of three proposed designs for supporting multiple APIs (see #1718). It takes a convention-over-configuration approach: Counterfact discovers API specs automatically by scanning a workspace directory. A lightweight YAML sidecar provides only what cannot be inferred from the file system.

## Summary

The user places all their OpenAPI documents under a single **workspace directory**. Counterfact scans that directory, pairs each spec with a generated-code subdirectory, and mounts all APIs on a single server. An optional `counterfact.workspace.yaml` file at the workspace root allows overriding inferred values without requiring a full config file for the common case.

### Example workspace layout

```
workspace/
  users/
    openapi.yaml        ← spec auto-discovered
    routes/             ← generated by Counterfact
      GET.ts
      {userId}/GET.ts
  products/
    openapi.yaml        ← spec auto-discovered
    routes/
      GET.ts
  counterfact.workspace.yaml   ← optional overrides
```

### Invocation

```sh
# scan ./workspace, serve all discovered APIs
counterfact --workspace ./workspace

# with overrides
counterfact --workspace ./workspace --port 3100 --serve --generate
```

### Optional sidecar: `counterfact.workspace.yaml`

```yaml
# counterfact.workspace.yaml
port: 3100
apis:
  users:
    prefix: /api/v1/users    # override inferred prefix
  products:
    spec: https://example.com/products/openapi.json  # remote spec replaces local file
    prefix: /api/v1/products
```

Only entries that need overrides need to appear. The `apis` keys match the subdirectory names.

## 1. Root path determination

Discovery proceeds in this order for each subdirectory that contains an OpenAPI file:

| Priority | Source |
|----------|--------|
| 1 (explicit) | `prefix` in `counterfact.workspace.yaml` for this subdirectory |
| 2 (OpenAPI 2.x) | `basePath` field in the spec |
| 3 (OpenAPI 3.x) | Path component of the first `servers[].url` entry |
| 4 (fallback) | `/<subdirectory-name>` (e.g. `users/` → `/users`) |

Conflicting prefixes produce a clear startup error listing the affected specs.

**OpenAPI file detection.** Counterfact recognises files named `openapi.yaml`, `openapi.json`, `swagger.yaml`, or `swagger.json` (case-insensitive). The first matching file in a subdirectory wins. Non-matching subdirectories (e.g. shared utilities) are silently skipped.

## 2. Generated code layout

Generated files land **inside the spec's own subdirectory**, co-located with the spec:

```
workspace/
  users/
    openapi.yaml
    routes/           ← generated routes
      GET.ts
      {userId}/GET.ts
    .cache/           ← compiled JS cache
  products/
    openapi.yaml
    routes/
      GET.ts
    .cache/
```

Each subdirectory is a self-contained unit. Types are generated relative to their subdirectory, so names like `$components/schemas/Pet` resolve independently in `users/` and `products/` with no cross-contamination.

## 3. Other implications

**Zero-config startup.** Running `counterfact --workspace ./workspace` with no other flags is enough to generate code and start the server for all discovered APIs. This is the lowest-friction path for teams that want to mock several services at once.

**Backwards compatibility.** The `--workspace` flag is additive. All existing single-spec invocations continue to work unchanged.

**Hot-reload.** Watching the workspace directory (`--watch`) triggers a re-generation only for the specific subdirectory whose spec changed.

**Adding a new API at runtime.** Dropping a new subdirectory with an OpenAPI file into the workspace triggers auto-discovery and mounts the new API without restarting the server (when `--watch` is active).

**Removing an API at runtime.** When a subdirectory is deleted while `--watch` is active, its routes are unmounted and its generated files can optionally be pruned (`--prune`).

**Swagger UI.** Each API gets its own Swagger UI at `/counterfact/swagger/<subdirectory-name>`. The dashboard lists all APIs.

**REPL.** The REPL gains a `:api <name>` command to switch the active API. Names come from subdirectory names (or an optional `name` override in the sidecar YAML).

**TypeScript type namespacing.** Because each subdirectory holds its own generated types, type identifiers are never shared across APIs. If the user imports types from multiple APIs in a single file, the import path is unambiguous (`../../users/types` vs `../../products/types`).

**Remote specs.** The sidecar can point a subdirectory at a remote URL. Counterfact fetches and caches the remote spec locally in `.cache/openapi.yaml` alongside the generated routes.

**Version grouping.** An API that has multiple versions can be represented with nested subdirectories:

```
workspace/
  payments/
    v1/
      openapi.yaml    ← served at /payments/v1
      routes/
    v2/
      openapi.yaml    ← served at /payments/v2
      routes/
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `--workspace <dir>` scans the directory and auto-discovers all subdirectories containing an OpenAPI file
- [ ] Each discovered API is mounted at its inferred prefix (with the four-step resolution above)
- [ ] A `counterfact.workspace.yaml` sidecar overrides inferred values per subdirectory
- [ ] Generated files land co-located with each spec in separate subdirectories
- [ ] Conflicting prefixes produce a clear startup error
- [ ] `--watch` re-generates only the affected subdirectory when a spec changes
- [ ] Adding a new spec subdirectory while watching mounts it without restart
- [ ] Single-spec CLI usage (`counterfact openapi.yaml ./api`) is unaffected
- [ ] The dashboard lists all mounted APIs
- [ ] `yarn lint`, `yarn test`, and `yarn test:black-box` all pass
